$comment: The screenshot traversal, copy-free. Each step is one action whose target
is an @anchor key (resolved via qa/selectors.json) or, for `type`, a literal.
This is the real source of truth — qa/flows/mobile.yaml is GENERATED from it
by compile-flow.mjs and must never be hand-edited.
Step verbs: waitFor | assert | assertNot | tap | tapPoint | type | pressKey
| hideKeyboard | swipe | wait | screenshot.  `screenshot` names map
to shot ids in qa/screenshots.config.json (the framer's source:
Two kinds of assertion, and the journey should carry BOTH:
- NAVIGATION sanity: waitFor/assert an anchor that proves a screen rendered,
so screenshots aren't of a splash and later taps land on the right screen.
- OUTCOME (Tier 2 e2e): assert/assertNot an anchor that proves the flow
produced the right RESULT - the new item appears after Add; the item is
GONE after Delete (assertNot); a total reads what it should. This is what
turns the screenshot run into a behavioral test. Aim for at least one
outcome assertion per core action (add / edit / delete). Both platforms
run the same flow in CI, so an outcome that only works on iOS fails the
Android job - the cross-platform parity tenet, enforced mechanically.

steps.$comment: OUTCOME assertion: the thing we just added is actually there.
OUTCOME assertion: after delete, the item is GONE (assertNot).
